text: [Music]
as dawn breaks over a movable city of
ten thousand yards queen borachen is in
for a rude
awakening a rogue sheep has slipped past
her servants and guards and bolted into
her yurt where he springs into bed and
bleeps in her ear
although she's a formidable catan of the
golden horde a huge kingdom in the
mongolian empire bharatiyan has a
hands-on approach to ruling
she's been married to batu khan the
fearsome grandson of genghis khan
himself since she was 15 and while her
husband is out on his raids she juggles
the duties of phlox family and empire at
home
this makes her the manager and the mover
of a city of thousands
twice a year borak chin moves the city
between two seasonal camping grounds
this ensures constant water and lush
grass in summer and protection from
harsh winds in winter
the whole operation requires weeks of
strict planning liaising with other
camps in her domain strategic delegation
and the patience to move at the speed of
dawdling animals
today is moving day and she'll have to
direct throngs of her ladies commanders
slaves and animals up the river vulgar
for the summer
as barak chin steps outside she's
greeted by a commotion
her unwanted visitor is now running
circles around her stewards they're
attempting to stow her possessions
securely into wagons
baractin orders them to get it under
control but she's the only one quick
enough to catch the stray
she next supervises her ladies who are
unpinning her yurt and lifting it onto
its custom wagon it requires a team of
20 oxen to pull and baractin wouldn't
trust anyone to steer it but herself
next bractin and her woolly companion
meet with the guards she ordered them to
keep close watch on her husband's
special reception yet and portable
throne during the journey they'll also
act as outriders and she tells them how
to secure the route surround her for
safety and keep the animals in check
but when the sheep finally breaks free
and makes for the fields the guard can
barely keep up as it scampers through
crowds packing up their yurts
exasperated bracton rides down to the
pastures herself
when she gets there she catches sight of
the troublesome sheep wriggling into the
middle of a flock
when she follows him in he's nestled
next to a you his mother she's pregnant
and seems to be in pain
with a start brakton realizes that this
used impending delivery has been
forgotten in the flurry of moving day
there's no time to find a shepherd
instead brakton rolls up her sleeves
greases her arm and helps the you give
birth to two new additions to the empire
leaving the lambs and their mother
braxton dashes back to the camp here the
final touches have been put to packing
and vehicles are starting to line up
this vast procession starts with the
queen and 200 wagons filled with her
treasures
next up are the junior wives and crew
then the concubines and this is only
baraction's camp
after this comes the second imperial
camp led by another senior wife then two
more camps also led by wives
brackton has been checking in with them
for weeks to ensure a smooth departure
and orderly queue but they only make up
the royal portion of the line behind
them winds the entire civilian city
which includes holy men with portable
chapels and mosques families tradesmen
and shepherds
finally baractin settles into her wagon
it'll take weeks to reach their
destination but over the course of the
journey she'll keep everyone expertly in
check from her proud children and
attentive subjects to the most
meandering sheep at the back of the line
[Music]
want to know more about baraction's
husband's grandfather learn more with
this lesson on history versus genghis
khan
[Music]
